programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A mother discovered her young son was missing from school. Within hours, the boy's entire family was frantically searching for him. But when the few leads quickly turned cold, the FBI was called in to assist in the investigation.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In January 1984, a U.S. Customs officer disappeared from his post near the Del Rio, Texas border crossing, prompting local police to search for four Latin American suspects. The following day, the official was found dead.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Boston, FBI agents tracked down a gang of robbers who specialized in armored car takedowns. As the robberies grew increasingly more violent, authorities launched a complex sting to trap the deadly ring of thieves.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A cross-country crime spree over a Labor Day weekend terrorized the nation. In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and teenage boy disappeared within hours of one another. The police suspected the cases were linked.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A special task force was formed in the early 1990s to combat the flow of Colombia cocaine into New York. As part of an elaborate sting operation, one detective went undercover and infiltrated a drug gang connected to Pablo Escobar's Medellin cartel.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1999, a brutal serial killer who traveled by train terrorized Texas. The FBI assembled a special task force to profile the violent drifter and help locate him.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Milwaukee teen was kidnapped by four gunmen who demanded $30,000 from his family for his safe return.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A drug dealer engaged in a shootout with Nebraska state troopers and evaded capture after a high-speed chase.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On July 4, 1987, seven inmates broke out of a high-security prison in New Mexico. The daring escape was orchestrated by convicted murderer William Wayne Gilbert, who was serving a life sentence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When an 80-year-old woman is found barely conscious next to the body of her murdered son in an abandoned van, the FBI was brought in and linked the crime to a teenager and middle-aged man.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1986, a psychotic killer shot and killed a police officer at point-blank range, leading investigators in a wild chase across the Midwest. As his tally of murders and abductions grew, the FBI raced against time to put a stop to the deranged gunman.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, Florida's cocaine imports were among the highest in the nation and regional territories were enforced with intimidation and murder. One dealer would stop at nothing to keep FBI agents from building a case against him.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A bank vault was blasted into from the ceiling, and over $2 million in valuables was missing. Agents were able to track down one of the robbers' friends and attempted to use him as bait to reel in the crooks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, Jamie McMahan and Christopher Kauffman went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1990s, a gang based in the U.S. Virgin Islands smuggled large amounts of cocaine into mainland America. Police and federal agents targeted the violent group, but its leader remained a mystery.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a series of bank robberies in Texas baffled authorities. But when similar thefts occurred the next year in California, FBI agents linked the crimes and established a modus operandi.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In June 1973, a young girl was abducted from a state park while camping with her family. A criminal profiler predicted that the kidnapper would contact the girl's family on the one-year anniversary of her abduction.

programme.desc: A father took his children out for food after murdering his wife, then strangled them when they returned home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of serial killer Geoffrey Evans and his accomplice John Shaw, who hit headlines in 1976 after the murders of two women in Ireland.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jerome Brudos was a serial killer and necrophile who murdered four women in Oregon during the 1960s. He was known as the 'The Lust Killer' and 'The Shoe Fetish Slayer.'

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Lonnie David Franklin Jr. is better known by the nickname 'Grim Sleeper.' He lured vulnerable women into his car before murdering and sexually assaulting them for more than two decades.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dayton Leroy Rogers, known as The Molalla Forest Killer, mutilated and killed at least eight women in the US.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Native American woman was brutally killed in the desert of New Mexico, and the crime scene was rich in forensic evidence: tire tracks, shoe impressions and even the murder weapons.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two boys are brutally attacked, investigators rely on evidence fished out of a nearby pond. It became the first case where diatom analysis was used to place a suspect at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of 16-year-old Leanne Tiernan was discovered nine months after she disappeared. Forensic scientists found clues that painted a virtual portrait of her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man disappears one year to the day after beginning an affair with his friend's wife. 15 years later, with the help of a metal detector, police find evidence that would prove what had happened and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer used poison which had no taste.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police recovered the car of a missing man, they expected to find his body - but it wasn't there.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A car carrying three men pulls up alongside another on an Alaskan highway and fires shots, leaving a passenger dead. One of the passengers in the killer's car agrees to testify against his friends. The resulting trials don't end the carnage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A high school gym teacher gives his friend a ride home but never returns; his wife and friend receive letters saying he started a new life, but they suspect foul play despite no body being discovered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man riding a bicycle was fatally injured, and police believed he was the victim of a hit-and-run accident. Tiny clues found at the scene created a picture of the vehicle which struck him and led police to its driver.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bruce Miller was shot to death in the office of a junkyard business. When a computer forensics expert examined the computers owned by the victim's wife, he found all the evidence needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter is the victim of a well-planned crime. Investigators suspect two weapons and two killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A nurse experiences a variety of flu-like symptoms. None of her doctors are able to discover the cause, until she visits the gynecologist for a routine check-up.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Just weeks before a witness is to testify against the man accused of sexually assaulting her, she is murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teenager went missing after an evening of horseback riding. Her body was found a month later, three miles from her home. The killer unknowingly left trace evidence behind - tiny but unmistakable clues which pointed to him and him alone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An obstetrician returned home from the hospital and found his wife on the bathroom floor. She was covered with blood and not breathing. He tried unsuccessfully to revive her, staining his clothes with her blood in the process, and then he called 911.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An employee of a dry cleaner was raped and murdered in the store. Police turned to forensic science for the answers they needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy real estate tycoon goes missing, it appears to be foul play. In his will, he leaves instructions in case he should die under violent circumstances - which were carried out after a hiker comes across his bullet-ridden skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Alaskan hikers discover a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Investigators find a tuft of red carpet fibers clinging to the sheet - threads of evidence which lead them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Investigators sift through the ashes of a fire which killed a 40-year-old woman. The fire appears to have been started by an unattended cigarette. On closer look, forensic scientists discover the cause was far more complicated.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a pipe bomb rips through a rural home, killing a young man and seriously injuring his mother, detectives hope that the remnants of a mailing label will lead them to a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of an Air Force officer was found dead in her bed with a plastic laundry bag near her face. Further investigation proved that the scene had been staged. Her death wasn't an accident, it was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A computer crash pushed a manufacturing company to the brink of collapse. Investigators must determine if the crash was the result of a sabotage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police investigate a discarded sleeping bag containing bloody sneakers and a purse, which eventually leads to the body of a young woman. A mark found on the victim's body enables police to track the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1986, Kathleen Lipscomb's body was found on a deserted road outside of San Antonio. Months passed, then years, and the crime was never solved.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a trio of unsolved murders occurred in Wichita Falls, Texas. Later, a fourth murder from the same time period provided the police with far more than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The only clue recovered from a shooting is a twelve-guage shotgun. Scientists use unique methods to lift the serial number from the weapon to trace it to its owner

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Prompted by news of a flight attendant's brutal murder, the son of a previous victim contacts police.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, Indiana police identified Richard Alexander as the "River Park Rapist" who assaulted four women that year. Later, DNA analysis casts doubt on his conviction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: California police had several suspects in the robbery and brutal murder of an elderly couple. Ten years later, a determined forensic scientist used material from a dentist's office to uncover ballistic evidence that had been overlooked.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer used poison which had no taste.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police investigate a discarded sleeping bag containing bloody sneakers and a purse, which eventually leads to the body of a young woman. A mark found on the victim's body enables police to track the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, Indiana police identified Richard Alexander as the "River Park Rapist" who assaulted four women that year. Later, DNA analysis casts doubt on his conviction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man who had committed crimes including murder was not caught. Almost 50 years later, advances in technology and handwriting analysis uncovered the criminal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating an apparent overdose suicide, detectives recover incriminating files from a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Just weeks before a witness is to testify against the man accused of sexually assaulting her, she is murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teen was killed by her parents in what they said was self-defense, but forensic evidence told otherwise.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Detectives and deputies arrest sex traffickers in a prostitution sting operation. A store owner reports a suspicious man looking into car windows. A deputy is called to a business where four people sitting in a car appear to be up to no good.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An inebriated man makes death threats after being removed from a bar. A deputy investigates a possible stolen vehicle in a grocery store parking lot. A man gives officers an unbelievable story.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a vehicle fire on a highway. A man claims local citizens have been stealing from his store. Officers assist at Lake Tahoe's SnowGlobe Music Festival. A young woman with a substance abuse problem escapes custody.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A theft at a supermarket ends in domestic violence. The sheriff's Homeless Outreach Team responds to a disturbed woman. A tip about a heroin dealer comes from a confidential informant.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of a man and woman fighting. Deputies investigate a reported verbal disturbance outside an apartment complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies are flagged down over an alleged case of domestic violence. A man armed with a shotgun claims that people on his property are out to hurt him.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A dangerous fugitive is wanted for assault and battery. A deputy stops an unregistered vehicle. A look at the daily procedures deputies must follow before going on patrol.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputy responds to a report of shoplifting. The search of a motel room yields incriminating evidence.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The investigation into a case of domestic abuse leads to the arrest of the abuser's friend on a $25,000 warrant. A deputy investigates two women who are parked behind a closed business late at night.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A probationer has a warrant out for his arrest. Two people are found sleeping in a car behind a drug store.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A vehicle has no front license plates and an expired registration. Two juveniles are reportedly intoxicated. Deputies are informed when a child predator is release from jail.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy goes after suspicious subjects in a parking lot. An intoxicated person has an emergency protective order keeping him from seeing his wife. A woman claims her boyfriend took her baby away from her and left.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy answers a 9-1-1 call about a lady stealing from a convenience store. A woman claims a prostitute solicited her husband in a restaurant.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a homeowner's report that people were stealing items from a neighbor's house. A woman is found driving her car with no license plate and a flat tire. A deputy is flagged down by another motorist to report an accident.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A theft at a supermarket ends in domestic violence. The sheriff's Homeless Outreach Team responds to a disturbed woman. A tip about a heroin dealer comes from a confidential informant.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police do a probation search of a known drug house. An officer gets information about a large marijuana grow operation. A deputy catches a drunk driver responsible for a hit-and-run accident.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A traffic stop reveals a man with stolen food and burglary tools who drives without a license. A deputy responds to a call about two intoxicated men. Two men are reportedly fighting at a department store.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An anonymous caller gives the whereabouts of a stolen vehicle. The driver of a car stopped at an intersection is unresponsive. A deputy responds to a report of a vehicle that may have been used in an earlier crime.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A bear is reported to be wandering near people's homes. Deputies respond to a domestic violence call.

programme.desc: Police respond to a report of a stabbing at a housing complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a 9-1-1 call reporting a verbal argument between a man and a woman.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A 9-1-1 call about a man threatening to kill his family. The investigation of a shoplifting case leads police to over 66 grams of black tar heroin. A domestic violence call.

programme.desc: Deputies struggle with a wanted man who had too much to drink. A deputy deals with two young thieves. A young man bleeding from the head gives an officer a difficult time.
